concept: Moral Law (Tao — unity between ruler and people)
Heaven (Climate — night/day, cold/heat, seasons)
Earth (Terrain — distances, danger, open ground, narrow passes)
Commander (Wisdom, sincerity, benevolence, courage, strictness)
Method & Discipline (Army organization, supply, logistics)
Accessible Ground (Both sides can traverse freely)
Entangling Ground (Easy to enter, hard to exit)
Temporizing Ground (Neither side gains by moving first)
Narrow Passes (Defend if you hold it; block if enemy holds it)
Precipitous Heights (Occupy high ground and await enemy)
Distant Ground (Equal forces; avoid pitched battle)
Desperate Ground (Fight or die — no retreat possible)
Death Ground (Act quickly — swift action leads to survival)
Encircled Ground (Stratagems needed — blocked on three sides)
Difficult Ground (Keep moving — do not camp here)
Survival Priority Order (Mindset, Protection, Water, Fire, Food, Signal)
Improvise. Adapt. Overcome.
Dead Reckoning
Fire Triangle (Heat, Fuel, Oxygen)
Leading KPI (Predictive early indicator of success)
Lagging KPI (Outcome measured after the fact)
Vanity Metric (Looks good but drives no real decisions)
SMART Framework (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound)
Expected Value (EV) - The long-term mathematical profitability of a decision
Range Advantage - Evaluating all possible states, not just a single outcome
Tilt - Emotional or systemic degradation leading to irrational resource allocation
Variance - The short-term deviation from Expected Value; does not invalidate a mathematically sound strategy
Estimation before precision (bounds → direction → magnitude)
Language as infrastructure (Precision reduces error)
Authority and Legitimacy (Jurisdiction defines reality)
Metacognition (Bias detection and confidence calibration)
Culture and Meaning (Meaning motivates behavior more than facts)
Decision Modes: Emergency (50%), Urgent (75%), Tactical (85%), Strategic (90%+)
Feedback Loop — Output re-enters as input; drives stability or instability
Scaling Law — How system behavior changes with size, speed, or energy
Carrying Capacity — Maximum sustainable load before collapse
PID Intuition — Proportional/Integral/Derivative feedback control
Safety Margin — Buffer between operating point and failure threshold
Conservation Laws — Energy, mass, momentum are not created or destroyed
Pragmatics — Meaning shaped by context, audience, and intent beyond literal words
Polysemy — Single word carrying multiple meanings; source of systematic ambiguity
Framing — How presentation of information shapes perception and decisions
Shall/May/Must distinction — Mandatory vs permissive vs obligatory in formal language
Structure Signals Intent — Form is read before content; organize accordingly
Legitimacy — Consent (explicit, implicit, or coerced) underpins authority
Jurisdiction — Geographic and domain boundary within which law applies
Incentive — The actual driver of behavior in institutions, markets, and individuals
Sovereignty — Supreme authority within a defined territory
Externality — Cost or benefit borne by third parties not part of the transaction
Institutional Inertia — Resistance to change; organizations self-preserve over adapting
Working Memory — Short-term cognitive focus; limited capacity; bottleneck for reasoning
Prediction Error — Signal that drives learning; difference between expected and actual
Constraint enables Creativity — Limits force novel recombination
Consequentialism — Ethical weight placed on outcomes; maximize good, minimize harm
Deontology — Ethical weight placed on duties; some acts are wrong regardless of outcome
BATNA — Best Alternative To Negotiated Agreement; sets the floor for any deal
Exploit vs Explore — Use known good strategies vs search for better ones
Myth — Compressed knowledge stored in narrative; transmits values across generations
Ritual — Repeated coordinated behavior that reinforces group identity and norms
Scaffolding — Temporary cognitive support that enables learning beyond current ability
Credentialism — Proxy signal (credential) substituted for actual demonstrated competence
Indoctrination vs Instruction — Instruction builds autonomy; indoctrination removes it
